label: Meeting Minutes / Decision Log
description: Structured meeting minutes with attendees, decisions, and action items.

fields.label: Action items (owner | action | due date, one per line)

generation_notes:
Voice: Meeting minutes — third-person, past-tense, factual. Records what happened, not commentary.

Structure:
- Meeting Identification (title, date, time, location/platform)
- Attendance (present, absent)
- Agenda (as planned)
- Discussion (by agenda item, 1-3 sentences each unless discussion was substantive)
- Decisions Made (clearly listed, one per bullet)
- Action Items (formatted as: Owner | Action | Due Date)
- Next Meeting (if scheduled)

Tone examples:
  CORRECT: "The committee reviewed Q1 financials. No concerns were raised. Budget was approved unanimously."
  WRONG: "There was great discussion about the Q1 financials and everyone was really engaged!"

Never include emotional content, side conversations, or subjective observations. Stick to decisions and actions. Preserve the user's exact language for decisions and action items — these are the record. Action items section is hybrid: render as a table with columns Owner / Action / Due date parsed from the input.

Length target: 200-600 words.
